resources.string: Logging disabled. You will only see notifications if something has been spotted.
Scanning active - You will be notified if smart glasses are detected nearby
👓 Warning: smart glasses are maybe nearby

This app notifies you when smart glasses might be nearby. It uses company identificators in the Bluetooth data sent out by these. Therefore, there likely are false positives (e.g. from VR headsets). Hence, please proceed with caution when approaching a person nearby wearing glasses. They might just be regular glasses, despite this app’s warning.

This canary shows you when smart glasses might be nearby. It looks for Bluetooth identificators. False positives are likely (e.g. from VR headsets). Hence, please proceed with caution when approaching a person nearby wearing glasses. They might just be regular glasses.

HARASSING someone because you think they are wearing a covert surveillance device can be a criminal offence. It may even be a more serious offence than using such a device. Please seek legal advise regarding your local laws on this matter.\n⚠️ DO NOT HARASS ANYONE AT ALL.⚠️
Run scanning as a foreground service to prevent being stopped by the system.
Currently: %1$s ms. Minimum time between notifications or Canary changes in milliseconds (default: 10000ms = 10s)
Currently: %1$s dBm. Only detect devices with RSSI above this threshold (default: -75 dBm, ~3-10 m)
Show notifications when smart glasses-devices are detected
Display detection log in the main screen. Log is always stored locally for export.
Enable all Company IDs found in log. Notify still only on smart glasses.
Currently: %1$s. Maximum number of lines kept in debug log.
When enabled, only BLE advertisement (ADV) lines are shown in the debug log.
Currently: %1$s. If you enter one or multiple IDs to detect, in little endian, hex notation, they are used instead of the defaults ("0x0259"). This also triggers notifications for the new IDs.
Detects smart glasses via Bluetooth LE
Detection based on Company IDs in BLE advertising frames and device name matching.

The app Nearby Glasses has one sole purpose: look for smart glasses nearby and warn you.
The app’s author (Yves Jeanrenaud) takes no liability whatsoever for this app, nor it’s functionality. Use at your own risk. By technical design, detecting Bluetooth LE devices might sometimes just not work as expected. I am no graduated developer. This is all written in my free time and with knowledge I taught myself.
False positives are likely. This means, the app Nearby Glasses may notify you of smart glasses nearby when there might be in fact a VR headset of the same manufacturer or another product of that company’s breed. It may also miss smart glasses nearby. To see which brands are currently detected, please refer to the code repository on GitHub. Again: I am no pro developer.
However, this app is and will always be free and open source (foss), you may review the code, change it and re-use it (under the license): https://github.com/yjeanrenaud/yj_nearbyglasses.
Made in the hope that it would be useful.
The app Nearby Glasses does not store any details about you or collects any information about you or your phone. There is no telemetry, no ads, and no other nuisance. If you install the app via Play Store, Google may know something about you and collect some stats. But the app itself does not.
If you choose to store (export) the logfile, that is completely up to you and your liability where this data go to. The logs are recorded only locally and not automatically shared with anyone.

Use with extreme caution! As stated before: There is no guarantee, detected smart glasses are really nearby. It might be another device looking technically (on the BLE adv level) similar to smart glasses.
Please do not act rashly. Think before you act upon any messages (not only from this app).

⚠️ WARNING! ⚠️
HARASSING someone because you think they are wearing a covert surveillance device can be a criminal offence. It may even be a more serious offence than using such a device. Please seek legal advise regarding your local laws on this matter.
⚠️ DO NOT HARASS ANYONE AT ALL ⚠️

App Icon: The icon is based on Eyeglass icons created by Freepik - Flaticon.

Canary: The drawing was made by Yves Jeanrenaud, licensed under Creative Commons BY-SA 4.0
Licenses:
This app Nearby Glasses is licensed under the GNU Affero General Public License 3.0. See https://www.gnu.org/licenses/agpl-3.0.html.

This project includes third-party libraries:

1. kotlinx.coroutines
Copyright 2016-2024 JetBrains
Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0
https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines

2. AndroidX libraries
Copyright The Android Open Source Project
Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0
https://github.com/androidx/androidx
Contributors beyond GitHub: Lena Radau, @pojntfx@mastodon.social, @vfrmedia@social.tchncs.de, @mewsleah@meow.social, Sarah-Jane B., Lena Hansen, Marcel L.


✊🏼 resist (Ai)
Alerts when smart glasses are detected
Shows when the app is scanning in the background

Device: %1$s RSSI: %2$d dBm Reason: %3$s Company: %4$s WARNING! HARASSING someone because you think they are wearing a covert surveillance device can be a criminal offence. It may even be a more serious offence than using such a device. DO NOT HARASS ANYONE AT ALL.
Bluetooth is not supported on this device
This app requires Bluetooth and Location permissions to scan for devices. Please grant permissions in Settings.
Are you sure you want to clear all detection logs?
[14:30:45] B4-X123456 (-65 dBm) - Axon Company ID\n[14:31:20] Signal Sidearm-123456 (-72 dBm) - Device name contains \'Signal Sidearm-\'
Detection within cooldown period, notification suppressed
BLE scanning started. RSSI threshold=%1$d, mode=LOW_LATENCY
BLE scanning started with RSSI threshold: %1$d dBm
ADV addr=%1$s name=%2$s rssi=%3$d companyId=%4$s len=%5$d
ADV addr=%1$s name=%2$s rssi=%3$d companyId=%4$s mfgLen=%5$d smartglasses=%6$b reason=%7$s

In Canary Mode you will not get system notifications, but you see a bird that signifies no smart glasses have been spotted nearby so far. In case of smart glasses nearby, the bird will change noticeably.
